description: MANDATORY non-UTC fixture (roadmap section 10): a UTC+3 user reviewing late at night. Asserts the due-date day boundary is computed from the review's tzOffsetMinutes (the device's local midnight), NOT from the UTC day. Where folding by UTC would give a different (wrong) due date, `utcFoldDueDate` records it and the runner asserts the divergence.

reviews.comment: 23:30 local at UTC+3 = 20:30Z the same calendar day; local and UTC day agree here, pinning the boundary math itself.
01:30 local on 2026-06-11 at UTC+3 = 2026-06-10T22:30Z: the UTC day is still June 10, the user's day is June 11. UTC folding would shift the due date a day early.
